claim: For all integers n > 1, sigma(n) is at least n + 1, with equality exactly when n is prime.
predicate: n > 1 and sigma(n) >= n + 1 and (sigma(n) == n + 1) == is_prime(n)

claim: If n > 1 is composite, then sigma(n) >= n + 3.
predicate: n > 1 and not is_prime(n) and sigma(n) >= n + 3

claim: For all n > 1, sigma(n) - n is at least 1, and equals 1 if and only if n is prime.
predicate: n > 1 and sigma(n) - n >= 1 and (sigma(n) - n == 1) == is_prime(n)

claim: For composite n > 4, sigma(n) - n is at least 4.
predicate: n > 4 and not is_prime(n) and sigma(n) - n >= 4

claim: If n is a power of 2 greater than 2, then sigma(n) = 2n - 1.
predicate: n > 2 and n % 2 == 0 and all(p == 2 for p in prime_factors(n)) and sigma(n) == 2*n - 1

claim: If n is a power of 2 greater than 2, then sigma(n) - n = n - 1.
predicate: n > 2 and n % 2 == 0 and all(p == 2 for p in prime_factors(n)) and sigma(n) - n == n - 1

claim: For any odd prime p, sigma(p^2) = p^2 + p + 1.
predicate: is_prime(n) and n % 2 == 1 and sigma(n*n) == n*n + n + 1

claim: If n is the square of an odd prime, then sigma(n) - n = n + 1.
predicate: is_prime(int(n**0.5)) and int(n**0.5)**2 == n and n % 2 == 1 and sigma(n) - n == n + 1

claim: For n > 1, if sigma(n) is odd, then n is a square or twice a square.
If n is divisible by an odd prime to an odd power, then sigma(n) is even.
predicate: n > 1 and any(vp(n, p) % 2 == 1 for p in prime_factors(n) if p > 2) and sigma(n) % 2 == 0

claim: If n is a square and not divisible by any prime congruent to 3 mod 4 to an odd power, then sigma(n) is odd.
predicate: n > 1 and int(n**0.5)**2 == n and all(vp(n, p) % 2 == 0 for p in prime_factors(n) if p % 4 == 3) and sigma(n) % 2 == 1

claim: For n > 1, if n is prime, then phi(n) = n - 1.
predicate: is_prime(n) and phi(n) == n - 1

claim: If n is prime, then tau(n) = 2.
predicate: is_prime(n) and tau(n) == 2

claim: If n is prime, then sigma(n) = n + 1 and phi(n) = n - 1 and tau(n) = 2.
predicate: is_prime(n) and sigma(n) == n + 1 and phi(n) == n - 1 and tau(n) == 2

claim: For n > 1, if n is a perfect number, then sigma(n) = 2n.
predicate: n > 1 and sigma(n) == 2*n

claim: If n is a perfect number greater than 6, then n is even and divisible by 4.
predicate: n > 6 and sigma(n) == 2*n and n % 2 == 0 and n % 4 == 0

claim: If n is a perfect number, then n is not a prime power.
predicate: n > 1 and sigma(n) == 2*n and not (len(prime_factors(n)) == 1)

claim: For n > 1, if tau(n) is odd, then n is a perfect square.
predicate: n > 1 and tau(n) % 2 == 1 and int(n**0.5)**2 == n

claim: If n is a perfect square and n > 1, then tau(n) is odd.
predicate: n > 1 and int(n**0.5)**2 == n and tau(n) % 2 == 1

claim: tau(n) is odd if and only if n is a perfect square, for all n > 0.
For n > 1, if n is a product of two distinct primes, then sigma(n) = (p+1)(q+1) = n + p + q + 1.
predicate: n > 1 and len(prime_factors(n)) == 2 and all(vp(n, p) == 1 for p in prime_factors(n)) and sigma(n) == sum(prime_factors(n)) + n + 1

claim: If n is a product of two distinct primes, then phi(n) = (p-1)(q-1) = n - p - q + 1.
predicate: n > 1 and len(prime_factors(n)) == 2 and all(vp(n, p) == 1 for p in prime_factors(n)) and phi(n) == n - sum(prime_factors(n)) + 1

claim: If n is a product of two distinct primes, then sigma(n) + phi(n) = 2n + 2.
predicate: n > 1 and len(prime_factors(n)) == 2 and all(vp(n, p) == 1 for p in prime_factors(n)) and sigma(n) + phi(n) == 2*n + 2

claim: For n > 1, if n is a power of a prime p, then tau(n) = e + 1 where n = p^e.
predicate: n > 1 and len(prime_factors(n)) == 1 and tau(n) == vp(n, prime_factors(n)[0]) + 1

claim: For n = p^e, sigma(n) = (p^{e+1} - 1)/(p - 1).
predicate: n > 1 and len(prime_factors(n)) == 1 and p := prime_factors(n)[0] and e := vp(n, p) and sigma(n) == (pow(p, e+1) - 1) // (p - 1)

claim: If n is a prime power, then sigma(n) - n = (n*p - 1)/(p - 1) - n.
predicate: n > 1 and len(prime_factors(n)) == 1 and p := prime_factors(n)[0] and e := vp(n, p) and sigma(n) - n == (pow(p, e+1) - 1) // (p - 1) - n

claim: For n > 1, gcd(n, sigma(n)) > 1 if and only if n is not prime and not a prime power.
predicate: n > 1 and (gcd(n, sigma(n)) > 1) == (not is_prime(n) and len(prime_factors(n)) > 1)

claim: If n is a product of two distinct primes, then gcd(n, sigma(n)) = 1 if and only if n = 6.
predicate: n > 1 and len(prime_factors(n)) == 2 and all(vp(n, p) == 1 for p in prime_factors(n)) and (gcd(n, sigma(n)) == 1) == (n == 6)

claim: For n > 1, if n is even and composite, then sigma(n) > 2n - 2.
predicate: n > 1 and n % 2 == 0 and not is_prime(n) and sigma(n) > 2*n - 2

claim: For even composite n, sigma(n) - n > n - 2.
predicate: n > 1 and n % 2 == 0 and not is_prime(n) and sigma(n) - n > n - 2

claim: If n is an odd composite number, then sigma(n) is at least n + 4.
predicate: n > 1 and n % 2 == 1 and not is_prime(n) and sigma(n) >= n + 4

claim: For odd composite n, the smallest possible value of sigma(n) - n is 4, achieved at n = 9.
predicate: n > 1 and n % 2 == 1 and not is_prime(n) and sigma(n) - n >= 4 and (sigma(n) - n == 4) == (n == 9)

claim: If n is a perfect square and n > 1, then sigma(n) >= 3*sqrt(n) - 2.
predicate: n > 1 and int(n**0.5)**2 == n and sigma(n) >= 3*int(n**0.5) - 2

claim: If p is prime, then phi(p^k) = p^k - p^{k-1} for all k >= 1.
predicate: is_prime(n) and k := 1 while pow(n, k) <= 500: phi(pow(n, k)) == pow(n, k) - pow(n, k-1); return True

claim: If n = p^k for prime p and k >= 1, then phi(n)/n = 1 - 1/p.
predicate: n > 1 and len(prime_factors(n)) == 1 and p := prime_factors(n)[0] and k := vp(n, p) and phi(n) == n * (1 - 1/p)

claim: If n is a prime power, then phi(n) + tau(n) = n - 1 + e + 1 where n = p^e.
predicate: n > 1 and len(prime_factors(n)) == 1 and p := prime_factors(n)[0] and e := vp(n, p) and phi(n) + tau(n) == n - 1 + e + 1
